candidates.name: Donald J. Trump

candidates.notes: Trump scores highest on education, self-defense, and America First categories. His weakest areas are marriage (accepts same-sex marriage, appointed openly gay cabinet member) and abortion (supports exceptions, opposes federal ban). His EO 14202 on anti-Christian bias and EO 14168 on gender ideology are his strongest Christian-aligned executive actions. His first-term bump stock ban and red flag law support cost him on self-defense.

candidates.name: Pete Hegseth
candidates.office: U.S. Secretary of Defense
candidates.notes: Hegseth scores the highest of all officials on this scorecard. His CREC (Christian Reconstructionist) denomination aligns most closely with the scorecard criteria. Perfect scores on self-defense and education. His explicit invocation of God-given gun rights, monthly Pentagon worship services, exclusion of Catholics from Good Friday service, and removal of transgender service members demonstrate the most consistently theocratic approach. His CREC church pastor has stated women should not vote. Moved family to Tennessee specifically for CREC church and school community. [2026 actions: As SecDef, reinstated all COVID-vaccine-discharged service members with full back pay (May 2026); ended ~80-year-old mandatory flu vaccine program (April 2026); implemented 2025 transgender military ban (continued separating trans service members through 2026). Confirms perfect T on industry_capture[q0] + family_child_sovereignty.]

candidates.name: Marco Rubio
candidates.office: U.S. Secretary of State
candidates.notes: Rubio scores highest on education (10) and self-defense (8). His complex religious journey (Catholic-LDS-Baptist-Catholic) and Cuban immigrant heritage distinguish him. His A+ pro-life rating is strong but he compromised on federal ban for Trump platform. His Gang of Eight immigration bill (2013) is his biggest vulnerability from this scorecard's perspective, though he has since reversed. As SecState reinstated Global Gag Rule on abortion funding abroad. His Cuban heritage means he does not fit the Anglo-Saxon preference criteria. [2026 actions: As Sec of State, bypassed Congress to send Israel $4B in arms via emergency declaration (March 2025); defended Operation Midnight Hammer (US strikes on Iranian nuclear sites); officially closed USAID July 2025 — 5,200 of 6,200 programs eliminated, remainder folded into State Department. Softened on China (cooperative tone at May 2026 Beijing summit). Confirms F on FPR q0 (Article I war powers bypass) + q3 (Israel-aligned + AIPAC-funded).]

candidates.name: Markwayne Mullin
candidates.office: U.S. Secretary of Homeland Security (sworn March 24, 2026)
candidates.notes: Mullin scores highest on self-defense (10), education (10), and abortion (8) - his no-exceptions abortion ban stance in 2022 is the strongest of any official on this scorecard. His Cherokee heritage is significant - he is the first Cherokee Nation member in the Cabinet. This directly contradicts the Anglo-Saxon demographic/heritage criteria in categories 5 and 7, giving him automatic false scores there. His judicial warrant requirement for ICE entry is more moderate than hardline enforcement would suggest. He voted against the Violence Against Women Act. [2026 actions: Sworn in March 24, 2026 as DHS Secretary. Departed from Noem's "flashy" detention-focused approach — walked away from warehouse purchases for ICE detention; ICE shifting back to judicial-warrant model for home entries. Joined ICE ops personally (VA, May 2026) arresting deported criminal alien. "Days of catch and release are over." Confirms perfect border_immigration record.]

candidates.name: Linda McMahon
candidates.office: U.S. Secretary of Education
candidates.notes: McMahon scores highest on education (perfect 10) which is her direct portfolio. Her prayer guidance for schools and patriotic curriculum partnerships are significant. However, she scores among the lowest overall because her WWE business background did not require taking positions on most scorecard categories. Her 2010/2012 Connecticut Senate campaigns required moderate positioning. She is focused on dismantling the Department of Education per Trump's agenda. The 'History Rocks!' tour promotes patriotic education. Null scores reflect absence of evidence, not counter-evidence. [2026 actions: DoED reduced from 4,200 → 2,300 employees (45% cut); offloaded 100+ programs to other agencies (elem/secondary to Labor, family engagement to HHS). Transferred $1.7T student loan portfolio to Treasury (March 2026). Office downsized + physical HQ move planned by August. Stated mission: facilitate transfer of DoED authority back to states. Consistent with strong school-choice + parental-authority record.]

candidates.name: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
candidates.office: U.S. Secretary of Health and Human Services
candidates.notes: RFK Jr. scores the lowest on this scorecard. Despite deep Catholic upbringing (rosary multiple times daily, mass twice daily in summers), his positions align with liberal Catholicism on most issues. His prior pro-abortion views drew opposition from Mike Pence's group during confirmation. He was confirmed 52-48 with only Mitch McConnell voting against among Republicans. His focus is on vaccine skepticism and health freedom, not the social conservative issues measured here. The Kennedy family legacy includes strong gun control advocacy. His environmental career was internationally focused. His Catholic faith is genuine but expressed through social justice rather than social conservatism. [2026 actions: As HHS Secretary, cut universally-recommended vaccines from 17 to 11 (January 2026; federal judge blocked overhaul March 16, 2026). FDA under his direction blocked publication of safety studies on COVID + shingles vaccines (May 2026). Hired David Geier to study vaccine-autism link. Confirms strong T on industry_capture[q0] (anti-mandate) + [q1] (anti-pharma-shield agenda).]

candidates.notes: Confirmed by Senate to lead the FBI under Trump T2 (sworn Feb 2025). Also acting ATF Director briefly. Former Department of Defense Chief of Staff (Trump T1). Author of "Government Gangsters" detailing alleged FBI/DOJ abuses. Hindu. Indian-American. Tied job security at FBI to purging agents linked to Trump probes (per fired acting FBI chief Brian Driscoll testimony May 2026).

candidates.name: Stephen Miller
candidates.office: White House Deputy Chief of Staff for Policy and Homeland Security Advisor
candidates.notes: White House Deputy Chief of Staff for Policy + Homeland Security Advisor in Trump T2. Trump T1 senior policy advisor. Founder of America First Legal. Lead architect of Trump immigration policy (travel ban, family separation, asylum restrictions). Jewish. Among the most influential non-cabinet officials in the Trump administration; signature issue is immigration restriction.
